title: Urban Heat Island Profile
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive cross-section visualization showing how urban land cover creates temperature differences, with tools to add trees, green roofs, and reflective surfaces

learningObjectives: Analyze how urban land cover drives temperature differences between cities and surrounding areas
Evaluate the effectiveness of different mitigation strategies for urban heat islands
Compare daytime and nighttime heat island dynamics
prerequisites: Understanding of basic energy concepts (absorption, reflection)
Familiarity with land use types (urban, suburban, rural)

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Interactive cross-section with click-to-modify land cover and real-time temperature feedback
keyQuestions: Why is the urban core consistently warmer than surrounding rural areas?
How does adding vegetation change the temperature profile?
Why does the heat island effect intensify at night?
Which mitigation strategy is most cost-effective per degree of cooling?
commonMisconceptions: Students often think the urban heat island effect is only about pavement being hot to the touch, not realizing it affects ambient air temperature across an entire region
Students may believe the heat island effect only matters during summer heat waves, when it actually affects energy use, air quality, and ecosystems year-round
Students sometimes think planting a few trees can eliminate the heat island, not understanding the scale of intervention needed
assessmentOpportunities: Have students create data tables comparing temperature reduction from different mitigation strategies
Ask students to design an urban heat island mitigation plan with budget constraints
Students analyze thermal satellite imagery of their own city using concepts from the simulation
